description: Authoritative state of known ME/CFS drug trials in Germany. Updated by the routine in ROUTINE.md. The first run after setup will (correctly) flag every relevant trial it finds as 'new', because this baseline starts empty. Do not hand-fabricate entries — only add trials confirmed from a registry or institutional source during a check.

trials.name: Observational Study on Immunoadsorption (IA) in Patients With Autoantibody-Positive Post-Infectious ME/CFS

intervention.name: Immunoadsorption (TheraSorb®)
intervention.mechanism: Removes GPCR autoantibodies (adrenergic, antineuronal) from blood via TheraSorb® affinity column (Miltenyi); 5 outpatient sessions over 2 weeks.

trials.sponsor: Charité University, Berlin (supported by German Federal Ministry of Research and Weidenhammer-Zöbele Foundation)

sites.institution: Charité - Universitätsmedizin Berlin, Institute for Medical Immunology

eligibility.summary: Adults 18–65 with post-infectious ME/CFS (Canadian Consensus Criteria), Bell Disability Score <60, post-exertional malaise ≥14 h, and detectable autoantibodies (adrenergic or antineuronal).
key_inclusion: ME/CFS per Canadian Consensus Criteria with PEM ≥14 h
key_exclusion: Conditions that preclude definite ME/CFS diagnosis

trials.notes: Immunoadsorption is apheresis (borderline for drug/pharmacological criterion) — included per ROUTINE.md: 'immunoadsorption is borderline → include + note it'; is_drug_study set to false. Status verified recruiting on ClinicalTrials.gov (April 2026). Contact: Dr. Elisa A Stein, elisa.stein@charite.de, +49 450 624354. Successor to the earlier Trial RIA (NCT05629988).

trials.name: MDC002 Phase 1 Clinical Trial in ME/CFS (planned)

intervention.name: MDC002
intervention.mechanism: Orally applicable small molecule; stimulates Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase and mitochondrial Na⁺/Ca²⁺ exchanger NCLX in skeletal muscle to interrupt calcium-overload cycle and restore cellular energy production.

trials.condition: ME/CFS, post-acute infection syndromes with exertional intolerance / PEM
trials.phase: Phase 1 (planned; pre-clinical stage as of mid-2026)

eligibility.summary: To be defined when trial opens; clinical trials are in planning stage as of June 2026.

trials.notes: Not yet registered in any trial registry. German criterion satisfied by German sponsor (Mitodicure GmbH). Pipeline page (mitodicure.com/pipeline/) confirms MDC002 is 'ready for GLP toxicity and GLP safety pharmacology studies' as of June 2026 — i.e. still pre-clinical; no Phase 1 registration or new funding announcement. Watch for Phase 1 registration on DRKS or ClinicalTrials.gov.

trials.name: Study to Investigate Improvement in Physical Function with Vericiguat Compared with Placebo in Participants with Post-COVID-19 Syndrome (VERI-LONG)

intervention.name: Vericiguat (VERQUVO™ / BAY 1021189)
intervention.mechanism: Oral soluble guanylate cyclase (sGC) stimulator — bridges endothelial dysfunction to improve microvascular perfusion and tissue blood flow. Developed for heart failure; being repurposed for post-COVID vascular dysfunction.

trials.sponsor: Charité – Universitätsmedizin Berlin (NKSG; German government funded)

sites.institution: Charité – Universitätsmedizin Berlin, Campus Buch (Experimental and Clinical Research Center, ECRC)

trials.condition: Post-COVID-19 syndrome (PCS), with or without fulfilment of ME/CFS criteria

eligibility.summary: Adults 18–50 with post-COVID syndrome (WHO definition) or post-infectious ME/CFS (Canadian Consensus Criteria), including post-COVID ME/CFS. Must reside in Berlin or Brandenburg. 104 participants randomised 1:1 to vericiguat or placebo for 10 weeks plus 30-day follow-up.
key_inclusion: Post-COVID syndrome (WHO) OR post-infectious ME/CFS (Canadian Consensus Criteria)

trials.notes: Recruitment started June 2023; 104 participants enrolled; planned completion April 2025. As of December 2024 (last ME/CFS Research Registry update) status was 'ongoing'. Results expected in 2026 (mecfs.de). Enrollment now complete — trial is active_not_recruiting (data analysis phase). Not in scope for enrollment but tracked for its results, which will be the first RCT of a vascular drug (vericiguat) in post-COVID/ME/CFS. Stratified by diagnosis (with/without ME/CFS) and sex.

trials.name: Phase 2 Study of Inebilizumab (Uplizna) in ME/CFS and Post-COVID Syndrome (NKSG/Charité)

intervention.name: Inebilizumab (Uplizna)
intervention.mechanism: CD19-directed monoclonal antibody; depletes B cells and plasmablasts (including pre-B cells), reducing production of GPCR autoantibodies. Approved for neuromyelitis optica. Being tested to eliminate autoantibody-producing cells in ME/CFS.

trials.sponsor: Charité – Universitätsmedizin Berlin / NKSG (funded by BMFTR, Nationale Dekade gegen Postinfektiöse Erkrankungen, up to €18M)

eligibility.summary: Planned for patients who responded to prior immunoadsorption (IA) — to select the autoantibody-mediated disease subgroup. Full criteria not yet published.
key_inclusion: ME/CFS or post-COVID syndrome with evidence of autoantibody-mediated disease

trials.notes: BMFTR announced Phase 2 funding in January 2026 as part of the Nationale Dekade gegen Postinfektiöse Erkrankungen (National Decade Against Post-Infectious Diseases, 2026–2036; €500M total). The inebilizumab trial is part of the NKSG multi-drug clinical platform at Charité. Design: only immunoadsorption-responders enrolled, to select for autoantibody-mediated cases. Not yet registered on DRKS or ClinicalTrials.gov as of June 2026. Watch for DRKS/NCT registration. European Commission approved inebilizumab (Uplizna) for generalised myasthenia gravis in February 2026.

trials.name: Semaglutide (GLP-1 Receptor Agonist) for ME/CFS Symptoms and Biomarker Identification (Scheibenbogen/Charité)

intervention.name: Semaglutide (GLP-1 receptor agonist)
intervention.mechanism: GLP-1 receptor agonist (same class as Ozempic/Wegovy); reduces body weight, modulates inflammation and immune function. Being tested in ME/CFS patients with overweight to assess symptom improvement and identify responder biomarkers.

trials.sponsor: Charité – Universitätsmedizin Berlin / Institute of Medical Immunology (Solve ME Catalyst Award)

sites.institution: Charité – Universitätsmedizin Berlin, Institute of Medical Immunology

eligibility.summary: ME/CFS patients who are overweight and receiving treatment for obesity. Aims to assess whether semaglutide improves ME/CFS symptoms and quality of life, and to identify biomarkers predicting response.

trials.notes: Funded by Solve ME's ME/CFS Catalyst Awards, announced February 24, 2026. PI: Prof. Carmen Scheibenbogen. Educational webinar held April 16, 2026 (slides available). Also includes patient-involvement component and biomarker discovery aim. Rationale: GLP-1 agonists reduce inflammation, may modulate dysregulated immune responses and vasodilation. Not yet registered on DRKS or ClinicalTrials.gov as of June 2026. Watch for DRKS/NCT registration.
